title: Dorsaly Field Research Campus publications
content:
Dane Zeller led a research group at the Dorsaly Field Research Campus in Rivermark that advanced subsurface acoustic tomography. The group's foundational results were published in 1862.

Theory.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Rivermark.

Apparatus.

Pilot studies in 1860 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.

Methods.

Annual conferences in Rivermark feature workshops devoted to safety protocols for field deployment.

Results.

Subsequent revisions added adaptive filtering that reduced noise during long-exposure runs.

Applications.

Applications include structural monitoring of bridges and precision alignment in turbine manufacturing.

Criticism.

Graduate instrumentation courses now teach the principles behind the 1862 measurement protocol.

Theory.

Criticism focused on sampling bias when instruments were operated without controlled humidity shields.

Apparatus.

Commercial licenses emerged after 2011, though academic use remains governed by a shared patent pool.

Methods.

The laboratory released open calibration datasets that other sites adopted within months.

Results.

The method relies on nanometer-scale arrays calibrated against reference beams from a sealed interferometer ring.

Applications.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Rivermark.

Criticism.

Pilot studies in 1860 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.

Theory.

Annual conferences in Rivermark feature workshops devoted to safety protocols for field deployment.
